name: ROK Financial
description_short: ROK Financial (ROKFI LLC) is a business loan broker based in Great River, NY that connects borrowers with lending partners across 11 financing products including term loans, SBA loans, equipment financing, CRE, and MCA, with a minimum credit score of 500.
description_long:
ROK Financial, legally ROKFI LLC doing business as ROK Financial, is a business financing broker headquartered in Great River, New York. Rather than lending directly, ROK Financial operates as a marketplace connecting borrowers with a network of lending partners across 11 distinct financing categories.

The product lineup spans term loans, business lines of credit, SBA loans, equipment financing, accounts receivable and purchase order financing, merchant cash advances, commercial real estate loans up to $10 million, franchise financing, residential investment loans, startup funding, and middle market lending. Most products range from $10,000 to $5 million, with commercial real estate being the exception at up to $10 million.

ROK Financial's most notable feature is its low entry barrier. The minimum credit score requirement is just 500 FICO, and businesses need only 6 months of operating history and approximately $120,000 in annual revenue ($10,000 or more per month) to qualify. This makes ROK Financial accessible to borrowers who would be rejected by most direct lenders and traditional banks.

Funding timelines vary significantly by product. Term loans and lines of credit can fund in 1 to 3 business days. Equipment financing takes 2 to 5 days. SBA loans require 30 to 45 days, and commercial real estate closings run 25 to 40 days. Each borrower is assigned a personal financing advisor who guides them through the application and matching process.

The company claims to have served more than 25,000 customers and displays the Better Business Bureau logo on its website. However, ROK Financial publishes zero pricing information — no APR ranges, no fee schedules, no rate examples. Borrowers cannot compare costs without submitting an application and receiving offers from ROK Financial's lending partners.

The broker model has trade-offs. On one hand, borrowers get access to multiple lenders and 11 product categories through a single application. On the other hand, there is no way to evaluate cost competitiveness upfront, and the intermediary layer adds a step between borrower and lender. The company's founding year is not disclosed, and its original domain rokfinancial.com now redirects to rok.biz, which may cause confusion for returning customers.
affiliate_program: Partner program for business owner referrals

tiers.name: Term Loan / Line of Credit
features: APR and fees not disclosed until application

certifications: Broker/marketplace model — connects borrowers with lending network

pros: 11 financing products under one roof — term loans, SBA, LOC, equipment, A/R, PO, MCA, CRE, franchise, startup, and middle market
500 minimum credit score is one of the lowest entry barriers in business lending
Startup funding available with just 6 months in business and $120K annual revenue
Commercial real estate financing up to $10 million, significantly higher than most online lenders
Personal financing advisor assigned to guide borrowers through the matching process
cons: Zero pricing transparency — no APR ranges, fee schedules, or rate examples published anywhere on the website
Broker/marketplace model means ROK Financial is not a direct lender — adds an intermediary layer
Company founding year not disclosed, which is unusual for a financial services firm
Original domain rokfinancial.com redirects to rok.biz, potentially confusing returning customers
Impossible to compare costs without submitting an application and receiving lender offers
best_for: Borrowers with lower credit scores (500+) who need broad access to multiple financing products from a single source
Startups and newer businesses (6+ months) seeking equipment, term, or SBA financing that direct lenders won't approve
Commercial real estate or franchise borrowers needing $250K-$10M who want a broker to shop multiple lenders
diagnosis: ROK Financial is a broker/marketplace connecting borrowers with multiple lenders across 11 product categories. Low entry barrier (500 FICO, 6 months) makes it accessible but zero pricing transparency means borrowers can't compare costs upfront. Best for borrowers who want one-stop shopping and may not qualify at direct lenders.
services: Term loans from $10,000 to $5 million for working capital and growth
SBA loans with 10-25 year terms matched through lending partners
Business lines of credit with 6-month to 10-year terms
Equipment financing with 1-6 year terms and 2-5 day funding
Accounts receivable and purchase order financing
Merchant cash advances for immediate capital needs
Commercial real estate loans from $250,000 to $10 million
Franchise financing for new and existing franchise owners
Startup funding for businesses with 6+ months operating history
Middle market lending for larger financing needs
Online application with personal financing advisor
typical_results_timeline: Application online. Term loan funding 1-3 business days. Equipment 2-5 days. SBA loans 30-45 days. CRE 25-40 days.
